title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: The clearest trust-and-safety signal today is around frontier-model oversight: independent predeployment evaluation remains the most concrete new governance mechanism in the list. The rest of the field is more mixed, with a few useful research inputs on interpretability, harmful-language detection, and real-world failure reports, but far fewer primary-source policy or standards updates than evaluation-focused ones.

items.title: METR publishes predeployment evaluation summary for GPT-5.6 Sol
items.why_it_matters: Independent predeployment testing directly informs deployment decisions, capability risk assessment, and external scrutiny of frontier models.

items.title: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol with an updated safety stack
items.why_it_matters: A new frontier model matters mainly through its stated safeguards, cybersecurity implications, and deployment-risk posture.

items.title: OpenAI backs shared standards for advanced AI
items.why_it_matters: Standards work can shape common evaluation, safety, and governance expectations across advanced AI developers.

items.title: Large-scale study analyzes user-reported breakdowns in AI healthcare chatbots
items.why_it_matters: Real-world failure reports highlight reliability and user-harm patterns in a high-stakes deployment setting.

items.title: New taxonomy targets indirect linguistic encoding for coded-language detection
items.why_it_matters: Better detection of obfuscated language could improve moderation robustness against evasion tactics.

items.title: Sparse autoencoder paper proposes regularizers for more interpretable top-k features
items.why_it_matters: Interpretability advances can improve understanding of internal representations used in model auditing and safety research.

items.title: Study examines interpretive labor in electronic monitoring systems
items.why_it_matters: It shows how human interpretation mediates automated surveillance outputs, affecting accountability and downstream harms.

items.title: Partnership on AI highlights LGBTQIA+ risks from AI bias
items.why_it_matters: Bias affecting protected groups remains a core deployment and governance concern for safety teams.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
